title: triticum vulgare var. compositum
intro: Triticum vulgare var. compositum, also known as wheat or wild wheat, is a subspecies of the common wheat plant, scientifically classified as a variant of Triticum vulgare. The medicinal properties of Triticum vulgare var. compositum include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Triticum vulgare var. compositum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and infusions, and it is also used as a base for herbal teas and tinctures.
